stem: An engineer takes 25 measurements of water flow rate using a flow meter. The measurements have a mean of 79.1 L/min and a standard deviation of 5.8 L/min. However, calibration tests reveal that a flow meter systematically underestimates the true value by between 3.2% and 7.2%. Estimate the true water flow rate and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 79.1 L/min. After correcting for the 5.2% bias: ≈ 83.4 L/min. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.2%-7.2%) and sampling variability: [79.4, 87.5]. Point estimate: 83.4 L/min.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
